summary: Qwen3.5-0.8B ultra-edge fine-tune (1ep vs 2ep) on the v0.2 corpus
config: training/configs/kubelm-edge-0.8b.yaml (1ep) and -2ep.yaml; config-only change from v0.3 path; same 561-record v0.2 corpus + recipe + Qwen3.5 render-fix

runs.label: 0.8B 1.5-epoch (sweet-spot attempt, 2026-06-01)
runs.release_gate: FAILED -- required beating 1ep on BOTH rubric (>24) AND fabs (<14); fabs improved (14->9) but rubric tied (24) and completion regressed (31->25). Not published.

findings: Fine-tuning works on the 0.8B: 1-epoch lifts rubric 19->24, ref 30->34 (beats both v0.3 and qwen2.5-7b on ref), complete 27->31. Schema-perfect, zero name/arg hallucinations throughout.

Underfit/overfit sandwich with a reasoning<->grounding trade: 1ep reasons better (rubric 24) but over-confident (fabs 14); 2ep grounds better (fabs 3) but overfit collapsed rubric back to untrained (19).
1-epoch is the keeper (rubric is the headline). The sweet spot is between (~1.5 epoch / lower LR), untested.

SWEET-SPOT HYPOTHESIS FALSIFIED (2026-06-01): the 1.5-epoch run did NOT Pareto-dominate 1ep -- it slid monotonically along the same reasoning<->grounding trade the 1ep/2ep pair traced. fabs moved toward 2ep's clean grounding (14->9, 2ep was 3) while rubric stayed flat (24) and completion eroded toward 2ep's collapse (31->25, 2ep was 24). No hump between the epochs; it is a slope, not a peak. More training buys grounding at the cost of termination. The remaining lever is lower LR at 1 epoch (flatten the trade), not intermediate epochs.

At rubric 24 the 0.8B is a legitimate ultra-edge tier (beats untrained) but not competitive with v0.3 (32) -- the gap is model capacity, not recipe. Judged WITHIN its 2-3GB CPU bracket, it is the right-sized bottom rung.
Both 1ep + 2ep errored on 1 scenario each (context overflow even at -c 32768 on a multi-hop scenario) -- residual non-termination.
artifacts: local only, unreleased (gated + not ship-ready): ~/kubelm-0.8b-artifacts (1ep), ~/kubelm-0.8b-2ep-artifacts (2ep). GGUF 517MB + adapter each.
infra_notes: fla/causal-conv1d fast path is a dead end on Unsloth+Triton-3.4 (broken binary on Ada sm_89; gated-chunk Triton crash on Hopper sm_90). Pure torch fallback only reliable path; only H100-class FLOPS tractable (~90s/step vs A40 347, RTX2000Ada 270). 'Exited by Runpod' was credit exhaustion, not platform fault.
